projectDescription: A runtime security layer for Node-RED that detects and blocks common attack vectors using a layered capability-based permission system without modifying the Node-RED core.
keyFeatures: Capability-based access control for Node-RED third-party packages
Runtime module interception via Module._load hooks to gate dangerous built-ins (fs, http, child_process, vm, worker_threads)
ES6 Proxy-based node isolation for every getNode() return value with dual-axis permission checks
Prototype hardening via Object.preventExtensions on all built-in prototypes before third-party code runs
Safe deployment review queue with visual flow diff UI
Network policy allowlist for restricting outbound HTTP/HTTPS calls by URL
Service Worker-based fetch interception for browser-side script isolation
Hardened Docker image with filesystem ownership split and read-only settings mounting
Offline Ed25519 license verification with no phone-home
Node-RED sidebar panel for managing grants and viewing threat logs
publicApiSurface: sentinel.allow — capability grants map in settings.js
sentinel.networkPolicy.allowlist — URL allowlist for outbound network calls
sentinel.license — license key configuration in settings.js
.sentinel-grants.json — dynamic grants file for UI-managed permissions
NRG_SENTINEL_LICENSE — environment variable for license key
NRG_SENTINEL_PUBLIC_KEY — environment variable for settings.js signature verification
NRG_SENTINEL_ALLOW_ESM — escape hatch for ESM packages during development
bin/node-red.js — wrapper binary that injects the preload and optionally verifies settings.js
plugin.js — Node-RED plugin entry point for sidebar UI and admin API routes
